questions.text: You are a loan officer at a commercial bank in Addis Ababa. A close family friend approaches you and asks you to approve their loan application even though they do not meet the eligibility criteria. What do you do?

options.text: Approve the loan since family relationships are important in Ethiopian culture
Politely explain that you cannot approve the loan because they do not meet the criteria, and suggest ways to improve their application
Ask a colleague to approve it so your name is not directly attached
Approve a smaller amount as a compromise

questions.text: Your organization has a strict policy against accepting gifts from vendors. A supplier delivering office furniture offers you a brand-new smartphone as a thank-you gift. What is the most ethical response?

options.text: Accept the gift but do not tell anyone
Accept the gift and declare it to your manager
Politely decline the gift and explain your organization's policy
Accept the gift and give it to a colleague so you technically did not keep it

questions.text: You work as a cashier at a government revenue office. A taxpayer offers you 5,000 Birr to reduce their tax assessment. What should you do?

options.text: Accept the money since government salaries are low and this supplements your income
Refuse the bribe and report the attempted bribery to your supervisor
Refuse the money but reduce the assessment anyway to be helpful
Accept the money and split it with your supervisor

questions.text: Which of the following best describes the role of personal integrity in a workplace?

options.text: It only matters when supervisors are watching
It means consistently acting ethically whether or not anyone is observing
It is a nice ideal but not practical in competitive work environments
It is only important for senior management, not junior staff

questions.text: You discover that your direct supervisor at an NGO has been inflating project expense reports to pocket the difference. What is the best course of action?

options.text: Ignore it because confronting a supervisor could cost you your job
Confront your supervisor privately and threaten to expose them unless they stop
Report the matter through the organization's whistleblower or internal reporting mechanism
Start doing the same since management apparently tolerates it

questions.text: You are an HR officer at a manufacturing company in Hawassa Industrial Park. During recruitment, a senior manager asks you to hire his nephew regardless of the candidate's qualifications. How do you handle this?

options.text: Hire the nephew to maintain a good relationship with the senior manager
Follow the standard recruitment process and evaluate the nephew on merit like all other candidates
Reject the nephew outright to prove you cannot be influenced
Create a fake interview process but guarantee the nephew gets hired

questions.text: Your company's data protection policy requires that all customer records be kept confidential. A friend who works at a competing bank asks you to share some customer account information. What do you do?

options.text: Share the information since your friend would never misuse it
Share only non-sensitive information like names and phone numbers
Refuse to share any customer information and explain that it violates confidentiality policies
Share the information but ask your friend to keep the source secret

questions.text: A colleague regularly arrives 30 minutes late to work and asks you to cover for them by clocking them in on time. What is the most ethical response?

options.text: Help your colleague because teamwork means supporting each other
Decline and explain that falsifying attendance records is dishonest, and encourage them to address their punctuality
Do it once as a favor but refuse if they ask again
Agree but ask them to return the favor when you need it

questions.text: You work in procurement at a government ministry. A vendor you have a personal financial interest in submits the lowest bid for a tender. What should you do?

options.text: Proceed with the evaluation since the bid is genuinely the lowest
Disclose your conflict of interest and recuse yourself from the evaluation process
Disqualify the vendor to avoid any appearance of impropriety
Ask a colleague to handle it but secretly guide their decision

questions.text: During a team meeting at your NGO, a colleague takes full credit for a project report that you both worked on together. What is the best way to handle this?

options.text: Publicly confront them in the meeting and accuse them of stealing your work
Say nothing and let it go to avoid conflict
After the meeting, privately speak to the colleague about giving proper credit, and if unresolved, raise it with your supervisor
Start taking sole credit for future joint work as payback

questions.text: You notice that petty cash at your office is not being properly documented. Small amounts go missing regularly, but no one is held accountable. What should you do?

options.text: Ignore it since the amounts are too small to matter
Take small amounts yourself since everyone else seems to be doing it
Bring the issue to the attention of your finance officer or manager and suggest implementing proper controls
Quietly start keeping your own records but do not report it

questions.text: Which statement best reflects a strong work ethic?

options.text: I do the minimum required to keep my job
I work hard only when there is a chance of promotion
I consistently give my best effort because I take pride in my work and feel responsible to my team
I work hard only when my boss is around to notice

questions.text: You are a branch manager at a microfinance institution. You learn that a loan officer has been collecting unofficial fees from rural borrowers who do not know the actual service charges. What action do you take?

options.text: Warn the loan officer privately and hope they stop
Investigate the matter, document findings, take disciplinary action, and implement measures to protect borrowers
Ignore it since the borrowers have not formally complained
Transfer the loan officer to another branch to remove the problem

questions.text: Your team lead at a textile factory asks you to sign off on a safety inspection report even though the inspection was never actually completed. What do you do?

options.text: Sign the report because your team lead instructed you to
Refuse to sign and explain that you can only certify inspections that have actually been completed
Sign it but write a private email to yourself documenting what happened
Sign it this one time but refuse if asked again

questions.text: Your organization's code of conduct prohibits using company vehicles for personal errands. On a Friday afternoon, a colleague asks to borrow the company vehicle to pick up their child from school. What do you do?

options.text: Lend them the vehicle since it is a small favor for a short time
Politely refuse and explain that company policy does not permit personal use of company vehicles
Lend them the vehicle but ask them to refuel it so no one notices
Drive them yourself in the company vehicle since you are technically still using it

questions.text: You work in the finance department of an international NGO operating in Ethiopia. You discover that a program manager has been submitting receipts for meals that never took place to claim per diem reimbursements. What is your responsibility?

options.text: Report the fraudulent claims through the proper internal reporting channels
Confront the program manager and demand they split the money with you
Mind your own business since it does not affect your department
Wait to see if an audit catches it naturally

questions.text: You are given access to the company's social media accounts as part of your marketing role. You notice that a personal post could get more visibility if you share it from the company account. What should you do?

options.text: Post it from the company account since more people will see it
Keep personal and professional accounts strictly separate and only use the company account for authorized business purposes
Post it but delete it quickly before anyone notices
Post it only if the content is not controversial

questions.text: Two of your team members at a bank branch are in a heated dispute. One accuses the other of deliberately misfiling documents to make them look incompetent. As their supervisor, what is your first step?

options.text: Ignore it and let them sort it out themselves
Immediately take the side of the more senior employee
Meet with each person separately to understand both perspectives before taking any action
Publicly reprimand both employees to set an example

questions.text: While processing payroll at a construction company, you accidentally overpay yourself by 10,000 Birr. Nobody else has noticed the error. What do you do?

options.text: Keep the money since it was the system's error, not yours
Immediately report the error to your supervisor and arrange for the overpayment to be corrected
Keep quiet and wait to see if anyone notices during the next audit
Donate the extra money to charity so at least it goes to a good cause

questions.text: Your employer requires all employees to complete an annual declaration of outside business interests. You recently started a small side business but it does not compete with your employer. Do you need to declare it?

options.text: No, since it does not compete with your employer
Yes, you must declare all outside business interests as required by the policy regardless of whether they compete
Only if someone asks you directly about it
No, because what you do outside of work hours is your private matter

questions.text: You are an accountant at a flower export company. Your manager instructs you to record a large personal expense as a business cost to reduce the company's tax liability. What do you do?

options.text: Follow the instruction since your manager has authority over financial decisions
Refuse to make the fraudulent entry, explain that it violates tax law and accounting standards, and escalate the matter if necessary
Make the entry but keep a personal copy of the real figures in case you need protection later
Ask for the instruction in writing before complying

questions.text: You have completed all your assigned tasks for the day with two hours remaining before the end of your shift. What is the most professional course of action?

options.text: Browse social media or watch videos since your work is done
Leave early without telling anyone
Ask your supervisor or colleagues if there is anything else you can help with, or use the time for professional development
Pretend to be busy by opening random documents on your computer

questions.text: You are a government employee and your department head asks you to use office supplies and the office printer to produce materials for a political campaign. How do you respond?

options.text: Comply since refusing could harm your career
Politely but firmly decline, explaining that using public resources for political purposes is unethical and likely against regulations
Do it after hours when no one is around
Agree but only print a small quantity to limit the misuse

questions.text: An employee at your bank discovers a system glitch that allows them to waive certain service charges without authorization. They tell you about it. What is the right thing to do?

options.text: Use the glitch to help customers who are struggling financially
Report the system vulnerability to IT and management immediately so it can be fixed
Keep the information to yourself in case you need to use it someday
Tell the employee to keep it quiet and not exploit it

questions.text: You are a project coordinator at an NGO. A beneficiary community leader offers you a cow as a traditional gesture of gratitude after a successful water project. Your organization has a strict no-gifts policy. What do you do?

options.text: Accept the cow to avoid offending the community and their cultural practices
Respectfully decline the gift, explain your organization's policy, and express gratitude for the gesture verbally
Accept the cow and donate it to another community in need
Accept the cow but report it as a project asset

questions.text: Your colleague confides in you that they are planning to resign in two weeks and asks you not to tell anyone. Meanwhile, your manager is about to assign a major three-month project to that colleague. What do you do?

options.text: Tell your manager immediately about the resignation
Say nothing since you promised confidentiality, even if it harms the project
Encourage your colleague to inform the manager themselves before the project is assigned, and explain the potential consequences of staying silent
Volunteer yourself for the project without explaining why

questions.text: You witness a senior colleague at your insurance company backdating a policy document to help a client make a claim they would otherwise not be entitled to. The colleague tells you it is a one-time favor for a long-standing client. What do you do?

options.text: Understand that customer loyalty matters and let it slide
Report the incident to compliance or management as it constitutes fraud regardless of the intent
Warn the colleague not to do it again but take no further action
Ask the colleague to involve you next time so you can assess the risk

questions.text: You handle sensitive employee records at a human resource department. A colleague from another department asks to see the salary details of a specific employee for personal curiosity. What do you do?

options.text: Share the information since you both work for the same organization
Share only the salary range without the exact figure
Refuse the request and explain that employee salary data is confidential and can only be shared on a need-to-know basis with proper authorization
Share the information but ask them to keep it private

questions.text: You are a warehouse supervisor at a manufacturing plant in Mekelle. You notice that a security guard has been allowing friends to take small quantities of raw materials out of the facility. What action should you take?

options.text: Ignore it since the quantities are small and the guard might retaliate
Document what you have observed and report it to management through proper channels
Confront the security guard and demand a share of the materials
Lock the warehouse yourself and handle security on your own

questions.text: You make a significant error in a financial report that has already been submitted to the board. You realize the mistake the next day, but no one else has noticed. What do you do?

options.text: Correct it quietly in the next report and hope the board does not compare versions
Immediately inform your supervisor about the error and submit a corrected report
Wait to see if anyone catches it before deciding what to do
Blame the error on the software or a junior colleague

questions.text: You are a team leader at a telecommunications company. One of your high-performing team members has been bullying a junior colleague, making derogatory comments about their regional background. What do you do?

options.text: Ignore it since the junior colleague has not complained formally
Address the behavior directly with the high performer, making clear it is unacceptable, and document the incident
Transfer the junior colleague to another team to remove them from the situation
Hint to the high performer that they should be nicer without being too direct

questions.text: You are responsible for shredding confidential documents at a legal firm. Due to a heavy workload, you consider throwing some old case files in the regular trash instead of shredding them. What should you do?

options.text: Throw them in the regular trash since they are old files that nobody cares about
Follow the proper document destruction procedure regardless of workload, or request additional time or support to handle the backlog
Tear the documents by hand since that is almost as good as shredding
Store them in your desk drawer to shred later when you have time

questions.text: You work at a development bank and are evaluating a major project proposal. You realize that the consulting firm that prepared the proposal is owned by your spouse. What is the correct course of action?

options.text: Evaluate the proposal objectively since you can be impartial regardless of the relationship
Immediately disclose the conflict of interest to your supervisor and withdraw from the evaluation process
Give the proposal a lower score to avoid any appearance of favoritism
Ask your spouse to temporarily transfer ownership to someone else during the evaluation

questions.text: During a company training session, you realize that most of the content is material you already know well. What is the most professional response?

options.text: Skip the rest of the training since it is a waste of your time
Attend but spend the time checking your phone and email
Attend fully, contribute your experience to help other participants, and stay open to learning something new
Complain to the organizer that the training is below your level

questions.text: You are a customs officer at Bole International Airport. An importer offers you a large sum of money to classify their goods under a lower tariff code, which would significantly reduce their import duties. What do you do?

options.text: Accept the money since your salary is insufficient for the cost of living
Refuse the bribe, classify the goods correctly, report the attempted bribery, and document the incident
Refuse the money but reclassify the goods as a goodwill gesture
Accept the money and classify correctly so at least the government gets the right revenue

questions.text: You manage a team at a hotel in Bahir Dar. Two employees are competing for a promotion. One of them privately tells you that the other has a criminal record. You have no way to verify this claim. What do you do?

options.text: Disqualify the accused employee immediately to be safe
Base the promotion decision solely on documented performance, qualifications, and proper background checks through authorized channels
Confront the accused employee with the allegation and see how they react
Promote the person who made the claim since they showed initiative by sharing the information

questions.text: Your company requires all business-related travel expenses to be supported by original receipts. You lost the receipt for a legitimate 2,000 Birr taxi fare during a work trip. What is the right approach?

options.text: Create a fake receipt to claim the reimbursement since the expense was real
Disclose that you lost the receipt, provide alternative documentation if possible, and follow the company's process for missing receipt claims
Inflate another receipt to cover the lost amount
Ask a friend at a printing shop to make a receipt for you

questions.text: Your organization asks all employees to complete an anonymous ethics survey. You have serious concerns about unethical practices in your department but worry the survey might not truly be anonymous. What do you do?

options.text: Give only positive responses to protect yourself
Skip the survey entirely
Provide honest feedback in the survey and also consider using other available reporting channels if the concerns are serious
Discuss the concerns only in informal conversations with trusted colleagues

questions.text: You are an IT administrator at a bank. While performing routine maintenance, you accidentally access a file containing executive salary information that you are not authorized to view. What should you do?

options.text: Read through the file since you already have it open
Close the file immediately, report the access control gap to your supervisor, and do not share or use any information you may have seen
Save a copy for yourself as leverage in future salary negotiations
Share the information with colleagues who are underpaid relative to executives

questions.text: You are a quality control inspector at a pharmaceutical company. Your manager pressures you to approve a batch of medication that did not pass quality testing because the company will face financial penalties for late delivery. What do you do?

options.text: Approve the batch since the financial health of the company is at stake
Approve it but make a note in your personal records in case there is a problem later
Refuse to approve the batch, document the failed test results, and escalate the matter to senior management or regulatory affairs if your manager insists
Suggest retesting the batch until it passes
